items.title: CVE-2026-1856: The Appointment Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cr...
items.description: The Appointment Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via custom booking field labels in all versions up to, and including, 1.4.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user ac
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-1856 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in the Appointment Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 1.4.4, due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in custom booking field labels. This allows authenticated attackers with Author-level access or higher to inject arbitrary web scripts that will execute when a user accesses the page. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or further malicious activities, affecting users of the vulnerable plugin. To remediate, update the Appointment Booking Calendar plugin to version 1.4.5 or later, and ensure that all custom booking field labels are properly sanitized and escaped. Additionally, restrict Author-level access and monitor user activity for suspicious behavior.

items.title: CVE-2026-12644: Versions of the package ts-deepmerge before 8.0.0 are vulnerable to Uncaught Exc...
items.description: Versions of the package ts-deepmerge before 8.0.0 are vulnerable to Uncaught Exception due to the improper handling of built-in Object.prototype methods (such as toString, valueOf). When user-controlled input contains these keys with non-function values, the resulting merged object becomes broken — any string context operation throws a TypeError, crashing the application.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12644 is a vulnerability in the ts-deepmerge package, affecting versions prior to 8.0.0, where improper handling of built-in Object.prototype methods leads to an Uncaught Exception when user-controlled input contains these keys with non-function values. This results in a broken merged object, causing any string context operation to throw a TypeError and crash the application. Real-world impact includes potential disruption of services relying on this package, such as email accounts, as indicated by the detected TTP T1586.002. To remediate, defenders should update ts-deepmerge to version 8.0.0 or later and ensure that user input is properly sanitized to prevent exploitation. Additionally, monitoring application logs for TypeError exceptions related to string context operations can help identify potential exploitation attempts.

items.description: The Blocksy Companion plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin settings in all versions up to, and including, 2.1.45 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with editor-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12430 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in the Blocksy Companion plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to 2.1.45. The vulnerability arises from insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in admin settings, allowing authenticated attackers with editor-level permissions and above to inject arbitrary web scripts. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the execution of malicious scripts whenever a user accesses an injected page, potentially resulting in unauthorized actions, data theft, or further exploitation. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the Blocksy Companion plugin to version 2.1.46 or later, and ensure that all WordPress installations and plugins are up-to-date. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual login attempts or changes to admin settings, and consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent XSS attacks.

items.description: The BetterDocs - Knowledge Base Docs & FAQ Solution for Elementor & Block Editor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the blockId attribute of the betterdocs/category-slate-layout Gutenberg block in versions up to, and including, 4.5.3. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the CategorySlateLayout::render() method, which echoes the b
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-12157 is a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the BetterDocs plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 4.5.3. The vulnerability is caused by insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the CategorySlateLayout::render() method, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts via the blockId attribute of the betterdocs/category-slate-layout Gutenberg block. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, including authentication certificates, as indicated by the detected TTPs T1649 and T1213. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update the BetterDocs plugin to version 4.5.4 or later, and ensure that all input fields are properly sanitized and validated. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent potential attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-11989: The Bit integrations – Form Integration, Webhook, Spreadsheets, CRM, LMS & Email...
items.description: The Bit integrations – Form Integration, Webhook, Spreadsheets, CRM, LMS & Email Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 2.8.7 via the upload_attachment. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to make web requests to arbitrary locations originating from the web application and can be used to query and modify i
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11989 is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the Bit integrations plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to and including 2.8.7. The vulnerability can be exploited via the upload_attachment endpoint, allowing unauthenticated attackers to make web requests to arbitrary locations, potentially leading to data breaches or modification of internal systems. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow attackers to query and modify internal resources, such as databases or file systems, potentially affecting sensitive data. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the Bit integrations plugin to version 2.8.8 or later, and ensure that all WordPress plugins and themes are up-to-date. Additionally, defenders should monitor their web application logs for suspicious activity and consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent SSRF attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-11752: A vulnerability has been identified in armeria-xds versions 1.38.0 through 1.39....
items.description: A vulnerability has been identified in armeria-xds versions 1.38.0 through 1.39.0, where DataSourceStream in the xDS module can resolve control-plane-supplied filenames and environment variables without restriction, allowing a compromised or semi-trusted xDS control plane to read arbitrary local files and environment variables on the xDS client host.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11752 is a vulnerability in armeria-xds versions 1.38.0 through 1.39.0, where the DataSourceStream in the xDS module can resolve control-plane-supplied filenames and environment variables without restriction. This allows a compromised or semi-trusted xDS control plane to read arbitrary local files and environment variables on the xDS client host, potentially leading to sensitive data exposure. The attack vector involves exploiting the vulnerable xDS module, which can be done by a threat actor using T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactics. To remediate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to armeria-xds version 1.40.0 or later, and restrict access to the xDS control plane to trusted entities only. Additionally, users should monitor their xDS client hosts for suspicious activity and implement measures to prevent unauthorized access to local files and environment variables.

items.title: CVE-2026-10779: The Classified Listing – Classified ads & Business Directory plugin for WordPres...
items.description: The Classified Listing – Classified ads & Business Directory plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all versions up to, and including, 5.4.2. This is due to a missing capability/ownership check on the gallery_image_update_as_feature AJAX handler (action: rtcl_fb_gallery_image_update_as_feature), which accepts a user-supplied listing ID and attachment ID and sets the feature
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10779 is a Missing Authorization vulnerability in the Classified Listing – Classified ads & Business Directory plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to and including 5.4.2. The vulnerability is due to a missing capability/ownership check on the gallery_image_update_as_feature AJAX handler, which allows an attacker to update any listing's featured image by supplying a valid listing ID and attachment ID. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized modification of listings, potentially allowing attackers to manipulate or deface listings for malicious purposes. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the Classified Listing plugin to a version later than 5.4.2, and ensure that all WordPress installations and plugins are up-to-date. Additionally, defenders can mitigate this vulnerability by implementing proper access controls and validating user input on all AJAX handlers.

items.title: CVE-2026-10720: Canonical MicroCeph versions from the squid and tentacle track are vulnerable to...
items.description: Canonical MicroCeph versions from the squid and tentacle track are vulnerable to a path traversal issue in the remote-import API. Holders of a trusted cluster mTLS certificate (such as enrolled cluster members) or join token can manipulate files in an imported remote cluster within the /var/snap/microceph confinement. This would allow daemon disruption and pollution of the cluster state.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10720 is a path traversal vulnerability in the remote-import API of Canonical MicroCeph versions from the squid and tentacle track, allowing holders of a trusted cluster mTLS certificate or join token to manipulate files within the /var/snap/microceph confinement. This can lead to daemon disruption and pollution of the cluster state, potentially affecting the stability and security of the entire cluster. The attack vector involves exploiting the vulnerability through the remote-import API, which can be done by sending a crafted request to the affected system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to a patched version of MicroCeph, ensuring that the remote-import API is properly validated and sanitized to prevent path traversal attacks. Specifically, defenders should verify that their MicroCeph version is updated to a version that includes the fix for CVE-2026-10720, and review their cluster configuration to ensure that only trusted entities have access to the remote-import API.

items.title: CVE-2026-10034: The WP DSGVO Tools (GDPR) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization by...
items.description: The WP DSGVO Tools (GDPR) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 3.1.39. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to supply an arbitrary victim email address and trigger immediate SAR processing via the process_now and is_ajax parame
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10034 is a vulnerability in the WP DSGVO Tools (GDPR) plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to 3.1.39, which allows for authorization bypass due to improper verification of user authorization. This vulnerability can be exploited by unauthenticated attackers to trigger immediate Subject Access Request (SAR) processing for an arbitrary victim email address. The real-world impact of this vulnerability includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update the WP DSGVO Tools plugin to version 3.1.40 or later and ensure that all WordPress installations are running with the latest security patches. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement measures to detect and prevent unauthorized access.

items.title:
CVE-2025-7737: DoS Vulnerability in 10G iSCSI Interface of Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform.


...
items.description:
DoS Vulnerability in 10G iSCSI Interface of Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform.



This issue affects Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E990, E1090, E1090H: before DKCMAIN Ver.93-07-21-80/00-05, CHB(iSCSI) Ver.88-01-02-04, before DKCMAIN Ver.93-07-01-80/00-07, CHB(iSCSI) Ver.88-01-02-04, before DKCMAIN Ver.93-06-82-80/00-06, CHB(iSCSI) Ver.88-01-02-04, before DKCMAIN Ver.93-06-63-80/00-04, CHB(iSCSI)
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-7737 is a DoS vulnerability in the 10G iSCSI interface of Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform, affecting versions E990, E1090, and E1090H with specific DKCMAIN and CHB(iSCSI) version numbers prior to DKCMAIN Ver.93-07-21-80/00-05 and CHB(iSCSI) Ver.88-01-02-04. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to a denial-of-service condition, impacting the availability of storage resources for affected organizations. To remediate, defenders should update their Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform to the latest version, specifically ensuring DKCMAIN and CHB(iSCSI) are updated to Ver.93-07-21-80/00-05 and Ver.88-01-02-04 or later, respectively. Additionally, network segmentation and traffic filtering can help mitigate the risk of exploitation by limiting access to the vulnerable iSCSI interface. Real-world impact includes potential disruption to critical storage services, particularly in environments reliant on Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform for data storage and retrieval.

items.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs
items.description: Japanese telecommunications operator KDDI Corporation disclosed a data breach where threat actors gained access to one of its email systems used by five other internet service providers (ISPs) in the country. [...]
items.ai_summary: The data breach at KDDI Corporation, a Japanese telecommunications operator, exposed up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs, including its own and five other internet service providers in the country. The breach occurred due to unauthorized access to one of KDDI's email systems, although the exact attack vector is not specified. This breach can lead to phishing attacks, email account takeovers, and other malicious activities, affecting the security and privacy of the impacted individuals. To mitigate this, affected users should immediately change their email passwords and enable two-factor authentication, and ISPs should implement additional security measures such as monitoring for suspicious login activity and enhancing their email system's security protocols. Users should also be cautious of potential phishing attempts and verify the authenticity of emails requesting sensitive information.

items.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
items.description: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to break into the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The systematic cyber attacks aimed at stealing sensitive
items.ai_summary: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) and the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) uncovered a long-running campaign by Russian intelligence services to steal messaging credentials from government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The attackers used social engineering tactics, specifically fake support texts, to trick victims into divulging their credentials. This campaign highlights the real-world impact of phishing attacks, which can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and potential data breaches. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all messaging accounts, ensure that all employees are aware of the risks of phishing attacks, and conduct regular security awareness training. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement incident response plans to quickly respond to potential security incidents.

items.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
items.description: An agentic coding tool tasked with cloning and setting up a seemingly benign GitHub repository could execute a malicious payload that remains invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human reviewers. [...]
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a malicious GitHub repository that tricks AI coding agents into executing a malicious payload. The attack vector is through a seemingly benign repository that is cloned and set up by an agentic coding tool, allowing the malware to remain invisible to security scanners and human reviewers. The real-world impact of this threat is that it could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, affecting organizations that rely on AI coding agents. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict validation and verification mechanisms for GitHub repositories and ensure that AI coding agents are configured to only interact with trusted repositories. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement measures to detect and prevent malware execution, such as implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) and keeping their systems and software up-to-date with the latest security patches.

items.description: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government. While Sol is the latest flagship model and the most powerful, Terra strikes a balance between efficiency and power, and Luna is fine-tuned for speed and affordability. items.title: Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites
items.description: Threat actors are selling investment scam templates created using the legitimate DCloud Uni-App toolkit. The post Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
items.description: The FBI and CISA are warning that a phishing campaign targeting Signal users tied to Russian intelligence services has evolved to steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys, allowing attackers to access victims' historical messages. [...]

items.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is giving federal agencies until Sunday to patch a vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server that is being actively exploited. [...]

items.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
items.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup Recovery Key. Hand it over once, and the attacker can restore the account's backup, read the private and group message history, and take over the account. Worse, the key keeps working.

items.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
items.description: A newly discovered cyber attack campaign has been observed delivering a previously undocumented malware family called SharkLoader that acts as a loader for deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon on compromised hosts. Kaspersky, which is tracking the activity under the moniker StrikeShark, said the campaign has targeted a diplomatic organization in Indonesia, government organizations in Taiwan,

items.description: Polymarket says it will fully reimburse customers who lost an estimated $3 million after hackers injected a malicious script into the platform's frontend following a breach at a third-party vendor. [...]

items.title: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
items.description: Threat actors are creating OpenAI tenants that impersonate legitimate companies and inviting employees to join them, in what appears to be a ploy to trick targets into submitting sensitive company information in chats and projects. [...]

items.title: Chinese-Speaking APT Deploys New TinyRCT Backdoor in Southeast Asia Campaign
items.description: A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been linked to a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT as part of cyber attacks aimed at government entities and critical infrastructure in Southeast Asia. items.description: AI won't replace GRC analysts, but it can eliminate much of the repetitive work they do. Anecdotes walks through building an agent that continuously monitors controls, identifies evidence gaps, and opens remediation tasks. [...]

items.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
items.description: A flaw in the Linux kernel's traffic-control subsystem can let a local unprivileged user gain root on affected systems. CVE-2026-46331, nicknamed "pedit COW," is an out-of-bounds write in the packet-editing action (act_pedit) that corrupts shared page-cache memory. A public, working exploit appeared within a day of the CVE assignment on June 16. Red Hat rates the flaw as

items.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
items.description: A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace, and Amazon Q does the rest. Amazon has patched it. Tracked as CVE-2026-12957 (CVSS 8.5), the bug sat in how Amazon's AI coding assistant handled Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Wiz

items.title: Nebulock Raises $25 Million for AI-Native Contextual Security
items.description: The cybersecurity startup provides threat hunting, proactive detection, and behavioral security analytics. The post Nebulock Raises $25 Million for AI-Native Contextual Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Exploited PTC Windchill RCE Flaw to KEV as Web Shell Attacks Continue
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a critical remote code execution vulnerability impacting PTC Windchill PDMlink and PTC FlexPLM enterprise Product Data Management (PDM) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability in question is

items.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets
items.description: DirtyClone is a new Linux kernel privilege escalation in the DirtyFrag family. JFrog Security Research published a working exploit walkthrough for the flaw on June 25, the first public demonstration for this variant. Tracked as CVE-2026-43503 (CVSS 8.8), it lets a local user corrupt file-backed memory through a cloned network packet and gain root. The patch landed in

items.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
items.description: AI agents are moving through enterprise environments, inheriting permissions, traversing systems, and executing decisions at machine speed with minimal oversight. The identity infrastructure built to govern human access wasn't designed for autonomous actors, and the gap between what enterprises are deploying and what their governance programs actually cover is widening fast. This guide breaks

items.title: Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites
items.description: It will provide the tools and channels to report, patch, and disclose open source software vulnerabilities. The post Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
items.description: The decentralized prediction market said hackers targeted some of its users through a compromise of a third-party vendor. The post $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets
items.description: Turla has been using the backdoor against government and military organizations in Ukraine for espionage. The post Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild
items.description: CISA has added the remote code execution flaw CVE-2026-12569 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The post First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges
items.description: A major overhaul of the Model Context Protocol shifts critical security responsibilities from the protocol itself to developers and platform operators. The post New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
items.description: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]

items.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
items.description: Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks. [...]

items.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
items.description: Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software. [...]

items.title: What do Ports Hear When Nobody's Listening? An Assessment of Automated Cybercrime [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 24th)
items.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Nicole Phillips, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

items.title: Linux Process Name Masquerading, (Wed, Jun 24th)
items.description: In a previous diary, I talked about stack strings&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;1&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5d; with a practical example of them. Since my SEC670 class, I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xe2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x80;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x99;m even more interested&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;in malware obfuscation techniques. I had&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;a look at process names. When you list running processes on a computer, can you trust what you see&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x3f; If you&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;re facing a rootkit, malicious processes can be simply hidden (the API calls or commands to list processed have been tampered). But a malicious process&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;can also mimic a non-suspicious name by

items.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
items.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

items.title: CVE-2024-40766: The Patch Fixed the Bug. Nobody Fixed the Configuration., (Tue, Jun 23rd)
items.description: The vulnerability

items.title: Webshells Remain Popular, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.description: Webshells have been popular for a long time. We already covered this topic across multiple diaries[1][2]. I spent some time to track them[3] and slighly paid less attention to them but today I found another one. It seems to be a new player (pushed on Github two months ago).

items.title: eBanking Phishing Delivered Through IPv4-Mapped IPv6 Address, (Fri, Jun 19th)
items.description: I detected an interesting phishing email this morning. It targets a major Belgian bank:

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
